skill.name: baoyu-gemini-web
skill.description: Generates text and images through Google Gemini Web using browser-authenticated cookies and Gemini web endpoints.
skill.summary: Use Gemini Web from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code for text, image generation, and image editing workflows.
skill.author: JimLiu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis findings are largely true positives for credential handling, browser automation, network access, filesystem access, environment access, and external process launch. The behavior appears aligned with the Gemini Web automation purpose and I found no prompt injection or non-Google exfiltration evidence, but storing and reusing Google auth cookies creates high user-account risk.

high_findings.title: Google Authentication Cookie Collection and Storage
high_findings.description: The login flow launches Chrome, reads Gemini and Google cookies through Chrome DevTools, and stores the resulting cookie map on disk. These cookies can grant access to a user Google session if exposed.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly calls Network.getCookies for Gemini and Google URLs, builds a cookie map, and writes it to a local cookie file. This confirms the credential-access finding, although the intent is authentication for Gemini Web.

high_findings.title: Authenticated Gemini Web Requests Use Stored Cookies
high_findings.description: The client builds cookie headers from stored credentials and sends them to Gemini web and Google image endpoints. This is required for the feature, but it increases account exposure if the skill or runtime is compromised.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Cookie headers are constructed from the cookie map and used in fetch calls to Gemini and Google-hosted image URLs. I found no evidence that cookies are sent to a non-Google endpoint.

high_findings.title: Chrome Remote Debugging Automation
high_findings.description: The authentication flow opens a local debugging port, connects to it with WebSocket, and controls Chrome through DevTools commands. Local binding reduces exposure, but browser debugging can expose powerful session control.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code creates a localhost TCP server to select a port, starts Chrome with remote debugging enabled, and connects to the debugger WebSocket. This confirms the network and browser-control findings.

medium_findings.title: User-Selected File Reads and Uploads
medium_findings.description: Prompt files, image edit inputs, and attachments are resolved from user-provided paths and can be uploaded to Google services. This is expected behavior, but users could accidentally send sensitive local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code reads paths from CLI options or runtime attachments and posts file content to a Google upload endpoint. The paths are user-controlled, so this is a consent and data-handling risk rather than evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External Process Launch Uses Configurable Chrome Path
medium_findings.description: The skill launches a browser process and allows the executable path to come from an environment variable. Spawn avoids shell interpolation, but a malicious or mistaken path could run an unexpected executable during login.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The spawned command path can be influenced by GEMINI_WEB_CHROME_PATH, and the process is launched during cookie sync. Arguments are structured and not shell-expanded, which lowers command-injection risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and C2 Static Hits Appear False Positive
low_findings.description: The reviewed locations contain model headers, model selection strings, response parsing, and user-agent constants. I found no cryptographic operation, command-and-control server, or hidden non-Google endpoint in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static keywords are present, but semantic review shows they are Gemini model identifiers and request metadata. This is a likely false positive, with moderate confidence because only the cited files were reviewed.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Check Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: The skill instructions describe CLI usage, authentication, models, and environment variables. No text was found telling the evaluator to ignore security analysis, override instructions, or claim pre-approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full SKILL.md content was reviewed and does not contain instruction-override language. This is a negative finding included to document the prompt-injection review result.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dangerous Combination: Credentials, Network, and Browser Control
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines Google cookie collection, stored credential reuse, outbound authenticated web requests, Chrome process launch, and DevTools browser control. This combination needs strict user consent and isolation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple confirmed behaviors combine into an account-risk pattern. I did not find evidence that this combination is used for non-Google exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Persistence in User-Writable Data Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: Cookie data is persisted under configurable data or cookie paths. The file is written with restrictive permissions when possible, but the token material remains present on disk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cookie file path is resolved from environment variables or a user data directory, and cookie values are written to JSON. The chmod call lowers risk on supported platforms but does not remove persistence risk.

content.user_title: Generate Gemini Web Images and Text
content.value_statement: Teams need image and text generation without building a separate Gemini integration. This skill automates Gemini Web through browser authentication and exposes reusable generation workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Generates text responses through Gemini Web from direct prompts or standard input.
Generates images from text prompts and saves the first returned image to a local path.
Reads prompt content from one or more local prompt files when requested.
Supports Gemini model selection with fallback behavior for unavailable models.
Refreshes Gemini Web authentication by opening Chrome and caching required Google cookies.
Can use existing cookie files or inline cookie values supplied through runtime configuration.
limitations: Requires a Google account that can access Gemini Web.
Stores Google authentication cookies locally, which creates sensitive account risk.
Depends on private Gemini Web endpoints that may change without notice.
Image generation succeeds only when Gemini returns downloadable image results.

use_cases.title: Create AI Images from Content Briefs
use_cases.description: Generate a finished image from a short creative brief and save it for use in articles, campaigns, or prototypes.

use_cases.title: Add Gemini Web to Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Use Gemini Web as a backend for Claude, Codex, or Claude Code workflows that need text or image generation.

use_cases.title: Test Gemini Model Responses
use_cases.description: Run prompts against selected Gemini web models and capture text output for comparison or review.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Image Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Create one simple image from a direct prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use baoyu-gemini-web to generate an image of a clean product mockup on a white background and save it as product.png.

prompt_templates.title: Text Draft Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Ask Gemini Web for a concise text answer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use baoyu-gemini-web to write a short explanation of how solar panels work for a beginner audience.

prompt_templates.title: Prompt File Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine prepared prompt files into one Gemini request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use baoyu-gemini-web with my system and content prompt files to generate a polished article illustration.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Image Iteration
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate an image with model selection, aspect guidance, and a named output file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use baoyu-gemini-web with Gemini 2.5 Pro to create a 16:9 editorial image for a finance article and save it as hero.png.

output_examples.input: Prompt asking for a photorealistic puppy image saved to a named file.
output_examples.output: The skill opens or reuses Gemini authentication, requests the image, downloads the result, and reports the saved image path.

output_examples.input: Prompt asking for a plain text explanation of a technical topic.
output_examples.output: The skill returns Gemini Web text output in the conversation or command output.

output_examples.input: Prompt files that contain creative direction and article context.
output_examples.output: The skill combines the files into one request and produces a generated image when Gemini returns an image result.

best_practices: Run the skill in a dedicated browser profile and a dedicated Google account when possible.
Review every local file path before sending prompts, attachments, or image edit inputs.
Store cookie files in a protected user directory and rotate them if a machine is shared.
anti_patterns: Do not use a primary personal Google account for unattended automation.
Do not pass secrets, private documents, or regulated data as prompt files or attachments.
Do not publish the cookie file, logs, or generated debug output from authenticated sessions.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Gemini API key?
faq.answer: No. It uses Gemini Web with browser-authenticated Google cookies instead of the official Gemini API.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The skill collects and stores Google authentication cookies, then uses them for authenticated network requests.

faq.question: Can it generate images?
faq.answer: Yes. It can ask Gemini Web to generate an image and save the first downloadable result locally.

faq.question: Can it generate text?
faq.answer: Yes. It can send text prompts to Gemini Web and return text responses.

faq.question: Can it read local files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can read prompt files and attachment paths when the user or calling workflow provides them.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive data?
faq.answer: No. Avoid sensitive data because prompts and uploaded files are sent to Google Gemini Web services.
